When Riverside Development Group handed us the keys to their 12-story mixed-use tower project in March 2023, their timeline was aggressive — 18 months from groundbreaking to handover. We delivered in 15.

Here's exactly how we did it.

## The Numbers First

<StatBlock stats={[
  {
    "value": "15",
    "unit": "months",
    "label": "Delivery time"
  },
  {
    "value": "3",
    "unit": "weeks",
    "label": "Ahead of schdule"
  },
  {
    "value": "$2.1M",
    "label": "Under Budget"
  },
  {
    "value": "0",
    "label": "Lost-time incidents"
  }
]} />

## Planning Before the First Shovel

The foundation of any fast project is the planning phase. We spent six weeks on pre-construction coordination before a single piece of equipment arrived on site.

<Callout type="insight">
  \*\*Key insight:\*\* Every week spent in pre-construction planning saved us roughly two weeks of reactive problem-solving during construction. This ratio held true on Riverside Tower.
</Callout>

Our approach involved simultaneous workstreams — structural engineering, MEP coordination, and facade design all running in parallel rather than sequentially.

## Site Progress

<BeforeAfter
  before="data:image/jpeg;base64,"
  after="/images/p3.jpg"
  beforeLabel="Month 1 — Excavation"
  afterLabel="Month 15 — Handover"
/>

## The Timeline That Made It Possible

## Lessons Carried Forward

The strategies that worked on Riverside Tower are now standard on every project over six stories. Pre-construction parallel planning, prefabricated MEP modules, and a single source-of-truth for schedule updates were the three biggest contributors to our early delivery.

We're applying the same playbook to our next project — a 20-story residential tower scheduled to break ground Q1 2025.
